title: In-Memory URL Shortener
description: Build an in-memory URL shortener that generates short codes for long URLs, resolves short codes back to original URLs, and tracks visit counts.
requirements: Generate unique short codes for given URLs
Resolve short codes back to original URLs
Track how many times each short URL has been resolved
Handle duplicate URLs (return existing short code)
List all stored URL mappings with statistics
Validate that input looks like a URL
hints: Use two HashMaps: one mapping short code to URL entry, one mapping original URL to short code
A simple counter with a prefix makes unique short codes (rs1, rs2, ...)
Check the reverse map first to avoid creating duplicates
Use get_mut() to modify visit_count in-place when resolving
For real apps, consider base62 encoding of the counter for shorter codes
bonusChallenge: Use base62 encoding for shorter codes. Add URL expiration (TTL). Add a simple HTTP server (using std::net::TcpListener) that handles redirect requests.
